name: Anglesey (United Kingdom)
description: A red field with a gold chevron between three gold lions rampant (two above the chevron, one below) — the arms attributed to Hwfa ap Cynddelw.
history: Registered by the Flag Institute in March 2014 following a campaign by Ymgyrch Baner Sir Fôn. The design is based on the arms attributed to Hwfa ap Cynddelw, a 12th-century ruler from Anglesey.

features.symbolism: the gold chevron of the arms attributed to Hwfa ap Cynddelw

features.role: three gold lions rampant, two above the chevron and one below
